mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue beats and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Joint index of INDPRO and HOUST shows zero predictive power for 20-day SPX returns (IC < 0.02) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, a decline in real consumer credit growth reduces household liquidity and dampens corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when RSAFS growth decelerates during Fed tightening periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no monotonic relationship with 5-day SPX forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01 over 5 years).

mechanism_narrative: A break in the industrial production index above 102.0 indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which historically triggers positive supply-side earnings revisions and drives equity gains over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of instances where INDPRO crosses above 102.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth acceleration and stable short-term interest rates signal a healthy labor market without inflationary pressure, which optimally supports equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term of PAYEMS growth and FEDFUNDS stability explains less than 1% of variance in 60-day SPX returns across walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sharp deceleration in housing starts signals collapsing demand momentum, which historically precedes negative equity returns over the next month as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not decline when HOUST falls below 550 while CPIAUCSL remains above 218 (t-test p > 0.05).
